findings.evidence: `SemigroupGroupPullback.lean:96-99` proves `comp_spatial_comp` for arbitrary `Φ` and `Ψ`, while `:102-104` specializes the same statement to `AddMonoidHom`.
findings.fix: Drop this concrete wrapper or restate it with `{Φ Ψ : Type*} [FunLike Φ W V] [AddHomClass Φ W V] [FunLike Ψ U W] [AddHomClass Ψ U W]`.
findings.issue: `comp_spatialAddMonoidHom_comp` restricts composed pullbacks to `(φ : W →+ V) (ψ : U →+ W)` even though the theorem it wraps is already homomorphism-class polymorphic.

findings.evidence: `SemigroupGroupPullback.lean:150-154` proves `comp_spatial_and_continuous` for arbitrary homomorphism-class `φ`, and `:144-146` proves continuity for any continuous function, but `:157-161` requires `(φ : W →+ V)`.
findings.fix: Drop this concrete wrapper or restate the packaged lemma at the same `{Φ} [FunLike Φ W V] [AddHomClass Φ W V]` level as `comp_spatial_and_continuous`.
findings.issue: `comp_spatialAddMonoidHom_and_continuous` packages only the `AddMonoidHom` case although both packaged components are already available for any homomorphism-class map with continuity.

summary: The core pullback/descent API is at the Mathlib homomorphism-class level, but two public convenience lemmas still expose unnecessarily concrete `AddMonoidHom` assumptions despite immediately deriving from adjacent general lemmas.
